revision.text:
Notice
This article is about a clan that is considered active. Any featured information may be outdated due to events in the clan. Feel free to correct any inaccuracies.
'''OutlawZ''' ('''[OwZ]''') == Clan description == === Goal === OwZ is a clan where everyone is equal. There are no leaders. There are no ranks. All the members are equally important to the clan, and all of them have an equal saying in clan decisions. It's basically what a clan should be like with no beurocracy, just members. This way everyone feel like they actually can do something and mean something for the clan apart from just playing cw's. This way we also can't have too many members in the clan, since it would become impossible to make decisions there. This way the clan wont radically change from day one to day two, since all the members in the clan have agreed on the structure and what it should be like, there are no need for changes, there are no need for new members, albeit we will of course be interested to have like-minded individuals joining us. We are not tryhing to raise an empire like some clans do. We are not trying to become omnipotent in LX. What we are trying is to, as time passes, be known as the best, made by the best, and remain the best for as long as possible. We have ambition and we have talent, we have an eye for talent and we have confidence. Withouth them you will get nowhere. So watch out for us, all of those things you, i and every lx player hige for, may very well become true for us. And once we have it, we sure as hell wont let it slip between our hands! === Main history facts === * September 16th 2006: OwZ is officially created by W.we, QuiverS, Swift and Hwoarang * October 18th 2006: Benn0 joins OwZ * November 2nd 2006: Gullveig joins OwZ * November 28th 2006 OutlawZ join LXA * November 30th 2006 QuiverS leaves OwZ to become joint leader in Vp * December 26th 2006 Magni Joins OwZ * Januari 2nd 2007 Kurt Joins OwZ * March 5th 2007 QuiverS rejoins OwZ * July 16th 2007 Odin Joins OwZ * August 1st 2007 Kurt and QuiverS Leave OwZ * September 16th 2007: OwZ one year day. Celebrates by refreshing the CW system, and inviting a new member to OwZ! The member is Tony Montana! * December 21th 2007 griffin Joins OwZ == Members == === Leaders === * - === Senior members === * - === Junior members === * - === Trial members === * - === Semi-trial members === * [OwZ] Swift * [OwZ] Wander * [OwZ] Benn0 * [OwZ] Gullveig * [OwZ] Magni * [OwZ] Rafman * [OwZ] Tony M. * [OwZ] Griffin === Not-even-almost-barely-semi-semi-trial-members === * [OwZ] Gaston == Rules == * Everyone is equal. * Members have their own boundaries. * We don't recruit, we invite. == The truth behind OwZ ''sponsored by Swift'' == The rulez are stolen from RIP. The equality is stolen from Osp. The skills are stolen from SLX. The members are stolen from many clans. Ding ding ding: We are just a RIP off [[Category:Clans]]

Notice
This article is about a clan that has been tagged dead. Any info is to be considered purely of historical value. Feel free to correct any inaccuracies.
'''[PK] Player-Killers''' was a LX clan which existed in September 2004. == Members == * - Purp Snail - * Eriis * Johnny * Barachetta * Psycho * EvilPhil * Kishiro Pro * Slurp == Additional information == Members were picked on this team for specific traits, that give us a combined advantage. It proved to be working very well. Slurp is our combo person (although non-combos well too) , to come in and wipe up everyone in the area with an insane burst of firepower Johnny is good at staying alive a long time, and steadily gaining kills. I am definitely the acrobat of the group, when I want to move, I'm gone, and usually someone is splattered all over the place too. Kish is very accurate, and can use rifle, and such weapons effectivley. Best at moving in, and efficiently taking enemies out, without hurting nearby teammates. I don't think I've ever seen kish make a TK. Eriis lets the people come to him. A master at small space combat, he lures noobs in and eats them, and he's my mod helper too. That was the idea I had when signing each of you on, in a nutshell. Im not out to find the perfect player like most "Uber we kicks yer arse n' fjsg WAH" clans (we all know what I mean). Im out to make the perfect team. Note- thats for the active people. Eriss is inactive, but his comp was taken, so he can't help it. == Settings == * Liero 1.10/1.11 * Shock 1.40 * Warhammer 40K 0.8 * Jail Break * Section 19A * Garrisons * Jukkedome * Liero Factory * Gamma complex == Rules == * No cursing * No combo [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Retired clans]]

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text:
'''Porting OpenLieroX to another system''' means to add support in the [[OpenLieroX]] [[OpenLieroX source code|source code]] so that it works on some other system. This can be a [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mobile_phone mobile phone] (or [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smartphone Smartphone]), a [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaming_console gaming console] or also another [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operating_system operating system] (right now, [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Windows Windows], [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linux Linux], [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MacOSX MacOSX], [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FreeBSD FreeBSD] as well as any [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/POSIX POSIX]-compatible system are supported).
= Information for specific ports = * [http://lxalliance.net/forum/index.php/topic,11522.0.html OLX on PS2] * [http://lxalliance.net/forum/index.php/topic,11505.0.html OLX on Wii] * [http://lxalliance.net/forum/index.php/topic,10802.0.html OLX on Nokia N800/N810] (working version reported) * [http://lxalliance.net/forum/index.php/topic,11167.0.html OLX on Motorola Z6] * [http://lxalliance.net/forum/index.php/topic,10917.0.html OLX on LG enV2] * [http://lxalliance.net/forum/index.php/topic,10760.0.html OLX on Xbox] (very unprobable to come) * [http://lxalliance.net/forum/index.php/topic,10313.0.html OLX on PSP]
In case you want to start a new port, please add it to this list and post some information in the [[LXA|forum]] or here in the Wiki.
= Basic requirements = == C++ == The most important requirement is that you can compile [[C++]] code for that system.
== SDL == [http://www.libsdl.org/ SDL] is currently used for:
* the input system (keyboard, mouse, gamepads) * creating the window / screen drawing area * handling system events (closing the game, etc.) * drawing on the screen/window
SDL is ported to a lot of systems already. Search on Google for an SDL port for your favourite system (because not all are noted on the official homepage). In many cases, having this will make the porting a lot easier. It also means that the system probably has all the requirements in principle to support OLX.
In case there is no SDL port for the system, you might also port SDL yourself. The SDL code is very portable and you will probably get help from many other people who are interested in it.
Getting rid of the SDL dependency in OLX would be a lot of work (maybe even more work than just porting SDL yourself). Though, it is not impossible and it has been made already much easier than it was earlier. The heaviest part is the graphics code and image manipulations. Though, this is also the part which is most likely replaced by [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenGL OpenGL] code at some time.
== OpenAL == Since OLX 0.59, we are using [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenAL OpenAL] for the audio code. (Earlier we used SDL for this. SDL is much more low level though and doesn't have 3D sound support.)
OpenLieroX should work with any OpenAL implementation. There is the [http://kcat.strangesoft.net/openal.html OpenAL Soft] implementation which should work pretty much on every system (or which should be easy to port).
== Boost == OpenLieroX uses some parts of [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boost_C%2B%2B_Libraries Boost]. Right now, that are:
* Boost signals * Boost process * Boost shared_ptr
== Some other libraries == Like [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zlib zlib], [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Libxml2 xml2] and others. I won't list them all here. But most of them are very basic and also pretty much cross platform themself. So even if your system doesn't provide this, the libraries are all [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_source Open Source] and will most likely be compileable on your system (if the rest of OLX compiles).
Take a look at the file ''DEPS'' in your OpenLieroX archive. That file contains a full list of all dependencies.
<!-- imported from Wayback Machine: https://web.archive.org/web/20120425164359/http://www.openlierox.net:80/wiki/index.php/Port_OpenLieroX_to_another_system -->

revision.text:
Notice
This article is about a clan that has been tagged dead. Any info is to be considered purely of historical value. Feel free to correct any inaccuracies.
== [pC] pugnaCitas (The Desire to Fight) == [pC] was a legendary LX clan which dominated the clan war scene during it's existence. The clan was thought to be a successor of [[Toilet Warriors]]. Though it consisted of many members, the great success of the clan was provided by the Kemp and QuafaS duo. == History == * 17th of June 2005 - the clan was created former [wc] Kemp, [Osp] QuafaS and [WaN] Morbid. On the same day Gyogyi joined. * 27th of June 2005 - Funkkarimies is granted [pC] trialship. * 28th of June 2005 - Drama, using the nick ''Dominator'', is granted [pC] trialship. * 18th of July 2005 - Drama and Funkkarimies become full members of [pC] * 20th of September 2005 - Former [wc] Rex joins [pC] * 7th of January 2006 - Turma and Chase join [pC] * 5th of February 2006 - [pC] loses their first cw against [[Let's Cheat Death]] and Gyogyi leaves the clan. * 18th of February 2006 - Inactive members are kicked. * February 2006 - [pC] loses their second ever cw against Experienced worms * 28th of March 2006 - Chase and Turma leave [pC] to create Half-Lifers * 16th of April 2006 - Drama returns to LX * 22nd of July 2006 - [pC] clan retires from professinal LX gaming == Members == === Leader === * [pC] Kemp === Co-leader === * [pC] QuafaS === Regular members === * [pC] Funkkarimies * [pC] Morbid * [pC] Drama === Prematurely retired members === * [pC] Rex * [pC] Gyogyi * [pC] Turma * [pC] Chase == Settings == === Mods played in clan wars === * Liero v1.0 100lt non-combo (limited weapons) * Liero v1.0 20lt combo * Marine Special Forces v1.22 100lt non-combo * Modern Warfare v1.0 100lt non-combo * 8-bit Warfare v1.2 100lt non-combo * Modern Warfare v1.0 20lt combo * Food Fight v0.6 100lt non-combo === Mods played for fun === * Liero v.1.11 100lt non-combo (limited weapons) * Trick or Treat v0.3 100lt non-combo * Shock 1.40 Air 100lt non-combo * Super Mario v0.3 100lt non-combo * Zelda v0.8 100lt non-combo === Levels played === * Poolhouse * Liero Factory / Liero Factory (Revisited) * Jail Break * Campus * LierosOfCaribbean * Jukke Dome * Castle Strike * Complex * Fossil Facility * wormmountain * S[aceStation * Cafeteria * destrdome * Neighbour wars == Standards of the clan == * Fairness and good gamesmanship * No laming other players * No multi-clanning * No cheating * No spamming * No camping * No running * No ceiling hanging == Additional information == * Everyone must agree if taking a new member (usually) * Regular members are allowed to start clan wars, but must let the leader know about it == Clan Wars == === Wins === Longest Winning Streak: 49 Overall: Approximately 90 === Losses === * [pC] vs [LcD] 0-2 (Liero v1.0, Liero Factory (Revisited), 20lt / Liero v1.0, Fossil Facility, 20lt)
[[File:LcD-pC.jpg|pC losing a 2v3 rifle game]]
pC losing a 2v3 rifle game
* [pC] vs [ew] 0-2 (unknown settings) * [pC] vs [Osp] 0-2 (unofficial tournament cw) === Draws === * [pC] vs [Osp] 1:1 (Liero v1.0 20lt x2) == Controversy == The reasons of [pC] retiring remain unsure. Inactivity for one was definitely a part of it. QuafaS and Kemp getting fed up with LX might have mattered too. Some speculate that argues between [Osp] and [pC] caused the fall of [pC]. This might be somewhat true, because [pC]'s certain actions in clan wars caused some annoyance among [Osp] members. This is also where [Osp] MileC gained his nickname ''MileC the clan destroyer''. Due to his argues with pC members Kemp declared that he's sick of LX. [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Retired clans]]

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text:
'''Rifles''' This is a setting that follows the rules of [[Combo]]. This setting is normally played on 20% [[Loading Time]] on the mod Liero v1.0. All weapons are banned besides '''Rifles'''. Sometimes some people unban Gauss Gun for some high recoil fun games. The weapons physics are what makes it so much fun. The recoil of the weapon is mild, and each shot will move the player back, and more so in the air. The damage of the rifle is 30 hp, it also has a few bit projectiles when the bullet hits a wall that can cause up to 6 damage if all hit. 3 bits, doing 2 damage each. There is a large controversy between Rifles and [[Mortars]]. Mostly because a lot of older players feels this because Rifles were around a lot longer than [[Mortars]]. They also dislike the [[Instagib|instagib]] aspect of Mortars. There are always some sort of debate around this subject on different forums (Currently most of it is on [[Liero Xtreme Alliance|LX Alliance]].) Unexperienced players usually plays a lot of mortars when they first start out. While most experienced players usually play other mods. There is also a new grouping of older players who plays both mortars and other mods. This has led to people who join servers and aren't known to the people on the server, who constantly requests mortars, are kicked out of most servers made by the more experienced grouping of LX.
Popular Mods
  [[Liero 1.0]] | [[Liero 1.11]] | [[Modern Warfare]] | [[Cruel]] | [[8-Bit Warfare]] | [[Food Fight]] | [[WH40k]] | [[MSF]] | [[Trick or Treat]]  


[[Category:Guides]]

'''Sausage Fighters''' ('''[H.K]''') (January 2004 - July 2005) was a Finnish and 100 % loading time playing clan. Several members of H.K clan are well known for their contribution of various popular Skins. == History == Clan was made by [[Jukke]] in the beginning of 2004. He was recruiting only players from Finland to keep the clan more active. In beginning of Fall 2004 Sausage Fighters got a bit inactive but never completelly stopped playing or died. You could still notice a 'Sausage Fighter' in game. In March 2005 [[Jukke]] decided to make Sausage Fighters active again. He made a contact with other members and together they would play more. Clan was active again until it finally died in July 2005. == Tag and name == H.K and Sausage Fighters came from "Helsingin Kauppiaat", a food company in Finland that makes sausages. == Controversy == Sausage Fighters were strictly Finnish clan which meant only Finnish players could join the clan. Such a rule was set in order to avoid time zone differences and increase the communication between the members. Clan was also against comboing and lower loading times. == Members == === Leader === * [[Jukke]] === Normal members === * Grande * Kama * Koin * Mr.Duck * Nappi * [[Pupsi]] * Sharun * Tuplanolla * Warheart * WarWorm == Rules == * [H.K] is primarily a non-combo clan. * All the camping and roof clinging crap should be left somewhere else. * No flaming wanted in [H.K]. * Members should register themselves and be active at least in H.K forums but preferably at GU too. == Clan Wars == === Won === * [H.K] vs (BtBs) (2:0) * [H.K] vs [BS] (2:0) === Lost === * [H.K] vs [TK] (0:2) * [H.K] vs (BtBs) (0:2) * [H.K] vs [wc] (0:2) * [H.K] vs [Osp] (1:2) * [H.K] vs [Osp] (0:2) === Tied === * [H.K] vs [East F] (1:1) * [H.K] vs [East F] (1:1) == External links == * [http://www.freewebs.com/hk_klaani/index.htm Sausage Fighters first home page] * [http://www.freewebs.com/sausagefighters/index.htm Sausage Fighters second home page] * [http://sausagefighters.proboards20.com/index.cgi Sausage Fighters forums] * [http://www.thegaminguniverse.com/forums/showthread.php?t=14537 Sausage Fighters GU thread 1] * [http://www.thegaminguniverse.com/forums/showthread.php?t=21355 Sausage Fighters GU thread 2] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Retired clans]]

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text: = Scripting Cheatsheet = Commands for scripting. === Files === ``` `` Main.txt = The main script text file, must be in a mod. w_*.txt = Usually a weapon script text file. p_*.txt = Usually a projectile script text file. `` ``` === Other === Order of variable settings does not matter as long as they are under the correct group. When leaving out a variable setting it usually defaults to 0 (else noted). It does not usually matter if you use capitals or not in the scriping. ``` `` [] = Different sections of the script code. = = Comment, all text after on same line will not be compiled. = ¬∞ = Angle, 360 is one full rotation. bool = boolean variable, can have value true or false. classic = the value in classic mod. def = default value, if you dont use this the value get the default value. float = floating point variable (decimals). int = interger variable (no decimals). less = must be less than that value to work. max = maximum working value of the variable. min = minimum working value of the variable. more = must be more than that value to work. ms = milli seconds (1000ms = 1s). px = pixels (with double resolution, which makes 320 is one screen in width). RGB = Red 0-255, Green 0-255, Blue 0-255 (eg 128,255,64). s = seconds. `` ``` * When using sound effect if first look for the file in you /sfx folder, if it does not find it there it looks in data/sound. * When using sound effect if first look for the file in you /gfx folder, if it does not find it there it looks in data/gfx. === Main File === ``` `` [General] = General mod settings. ModName = Name of the mod that will show up in the mod list. If you use more than one word you must incapselate it with "" when you compile. [Weapons] = Weapon list settings. NumWeapons = Number of weapons the mod contains (int, min 1). Weapon# = File name of a weapon. Replace # with an interger for each weapons. Use same amount of lines as the NumWeapons is. Weapon1 shows up first in the list when you choose weapons, Weapon2 comes in second place and so on. If a weapon a filename is wrong or miss a Weapon# the weapon will be blank, but the game will still run with defualt values on the weapon. [NinjaRope] = The Ninja Rope setting. RopeLength = The max lenght on the rope when you throw in px (int, classic 300). Note, you can max throw the rope about 900 in right up in the air. RestLength = The min lenght the rope will have when you hang by it in px (int, classic 27). Strength = How hard it will drag you until you have reached min length (float, classic 4.5). [Worm] = Settings on all worms. AngleSpeed = How fast it can change direction of the aim (classic 150, less is slower). GroundSpeed = How fast it moves on land (classic 8, less is slower). AirSpeed = How fast it moves in air (classic 4, less is slower). Note: A bug makes air speed is also used on ground. Negative walues will give some strange results on both speeds. Gravity = How much the gravity affect it, (classic 100, 0 is no gravity, and negative number will make it moves upwards instead). JumpForce = How fast it will jump (classic -65, possetive will make it bounce first). AirFriction = How much the air slows it down (float, min 0, max 1, classic 0). GroundFriction = How much ground slows it down (float, min 0, max 1, classic 0.4). `` ``` === Weapon File === ``` `` [General] = Must be in a weapon/projectile scrip file. Main settings. Name = Name that will show up in weapon list. Type = Use "Type = WPN_BEAM" of you want to use beam weapon. *Class = Can be: WCL_AUTOMATIC WCL_CLOSERANGE WCL_GRENADE WCL_MISSILE, WCL_POWERGUN. Just used for future reference. Recoil = How much you will fly backwards when you shoot. Negative will make you fly forward instead. Recharge = Time it takes to reload weapon the more the faster (float, min 0, def 0, 10s/number). Example: 1 = 10s, 0.5 = 20s, 2 = 5s, 10 = 1s. Drain = How much each shot will drain in percent. When more than 100 have been used you must reload to shoot again. Note the "more", if it have drained 100 you can still shoot one more time, it needs to be just over it for just one shot, eg 100.001. ROF = Rate of Fire, delay between shots in same clip, in ms (int, min 1, def 1). Sound = A sound file that will be played when the weapon is fired. LaserSight = Shows a dottet line directly at where the player aims. [Beam] = Creates a beam with some setings. Ignores Recoil, Sound and LaserSight in General. Damage = Size of the damage on dirt (int). PlayerDamage = Damage player will take when hit (int). Colour = Color of the whole beam (RGB). Length = Length of the beam in px (int, use 999999 to have no end of the beam). `` ``` === Weapon and Projectile Files === ``` `` [Projectile] = Creates a projectile with some settings. Amount = How many projectiles that will be created each time (int, min 1, max 2047, def 0). Speed = How fast the projectiles moves (def 0). More than 1000 can usually pierce through materials. Moves backwards with a negative number. SpeedVar = Randomly extra or less in speed (def 0). Example 10 can give 0-10 extra speed or 0-10 less speed. *Spread = How much randlomly each projectile can spread at both direction from the middle in ¬∞ (float, min 0, max 180). In v0.55b 0 spread works correctly. Projectile = The name of the projectile file that will be loaded. Angle = In wich direction the projectile start to move in ¬∞, 0 is to the right, 90 is down, -90 is up, 180 is left. Variable ignored in the weapons first projectiles and in trail. Useangle = Bool (def false), when true use last projectiles angle (+ Angle variable) instead of using absolute angle. Variable ignored in the weapons first projectiles and in trail. `` ``` === Projectile File === ``` `` [General] = Main settings on the projectile. Type = Can have values: PRJ_IMAGE or PRJ_PIXEL. PRJ_IMAGE followed by Image in new line to use an image, PRJ_PIXEL followed by Colour1 and optional Colour2 if you want pixel graphics. Image = Name of the image file that will be used on the projectile. Colour1 = The RGB value of the pixels (def black). Colour2 = Use this if you want some variation on the pixel colors (def not used). Trail = Everything but TRL_PROJECTILE and TRL_NONE use hard coded images. Can have values: TRL_CHEMSMOKE = Green smoke effects. TRL_DOOMSDAY = tiny fire trail. TRL_EXPLOSIVE = big explosion trail. TRL_NONE = have no trail. TRL_PROJECTILE = have a trail that is defined in [ProjectileTrail]. TRL_SMOKE = use the smoke.png trail (hardcoded). Gravity = How much gravity effects the projectile (0 = nothing, 100 = full, 200 = double effect, negative value will make it moves upwards instead of downwards). Dampening = Multiplies with projectile speed each frame (float, 1.0 = no effect, less than 1.0 slows it down, more than 1.0 accelerate the projectile, def 1.0). Timer = In how many seconds the [Time] trigger will be used, in s (float). *TimerVar = Radom time length variance. Rotating = Bool (def false). If the projectile will rotate when it moves. If true also use RotIncrement and RotSpeed. *RotIncrement = How many rotated images there is in the file in ¬∞. For 4 direction 90, for 8 45, for 16 23. RotSpeed = How fast the rotation will be, the higher value the faster (int). Also rotates slower the slower the projectile moves. UseAngle = Bool (def false), when true the graphics will be dependent on the projectiles angle, If true also use AngleImages. UseSpecAngle = Don't know the difference between this and UseAngle except it show some directions wrong. AngleImages = Tells how many direction images there is in the file. These amount of directions seams to work best: 10, 18, 34, 66. Animating = Bool, when true use also AnimRate and AnimType AnimRate = How long each frame in the animation will be shown, the less the slower. AnimType = Can have values ANI_ONCE, ANI_LOOP, ANI_PINGPONG. ANI_ONCE = Animate from left to right then the projectile dissaperes. ANI_LOOP = Animate from left to right again and again until projectile dissaperes. ANI_PINGPONG = Animate from left to right and back again then dissaperes. [Hit] = Setting on what will happen when a projectile hits a wall (both dirt and rock). Type = Can have values: Bounce, Carve, Dirt, Explosion, GreenDirt, Nothing (def Nothing). Bounce = will make the projectile bounce on the material (use BounceCoeff too). Carve = damage walls without showing some animation. Dirt = will make 3 dirt circles around it. Explode = use the "Explosion.png" in different sizes depending on damage. GreenDirt = makes a tiny green dirt. Nothing = will make the projectile moves through dirt and rock (max speed around 50). Damage = How much that will be removed on dirt (int, def 0). In LieroX v0.50b it also damage the player in same damage (instead of just 1 as it was before). Maximum damage is 15 (image bug if higher) Use Damage = -1 if you don't want to show a small explosion image. BounceCoeff = How much the projectile will loose in speed when it bounce (float, 1.0 = no speed loss, 0.0 all speed loss, over 1.0 it gets faster after each bounce). BounceExplode = If the projectile explodes every time it bounce (int, the higher, the bigger removal). If it is slow or have low angle it can carve through much dirt (a bug that it gets faster when it bounces). Projectiles = Bool (def false). If true and "Type = Explosion" the [Projectile] will trigger. Shake = How much the whole level will shake (def 0). Sound = Sound file that will be used. [PlayerHit] = What will happen when a projectile hits a player (both computer and human). Only the Type variale works differently compared to [Hit]. Type = Can have values: Explosion, Injure, Nothing (def Nothing). Explode with show exploding animation but max do 5 damage. Injure will hurt the player directly with no max damage. Nothing will just pass though the player (but the same time also ignore wall hits). Both Explode and Injure can trigger a [Projectile]. [Time] = What will happen when a projectile have a certain age. Using same variables like [Hit] and [PlayerHit] with an exception of Sound that unfortunatly does not work. [ProjectileTrail] = When "Trail = TRL_PROJECTILE" is stated in [General]. UseProjVelocity = Bool, true if the trail will use the speed of the projectile it shoots from. Delay = Delay between each projectile in the trail, in ms `` ``` [[Category:Guides]]

revision.text:
'''Sheep Wars''' (April 22, 2005 - May 23, 2005) was a tournament inspired by [[Jukke]]'s The Sheep Story Comics and hosted by Avye. It was planed to have several phases as a The Sheep Story Comics adaptation but it ended after the first one because of lack of the interest and activity. == Plot == === Phase 1: Primal Aggression (April 27, 2005) === The Sheep and Lieros first come out of existence; and the Sheep decide that living in the ground is NOT for them! The curious Lieros follow the Sheep up to the land above, and soon the Lieros become jealous of the Sheep! War breaks out between the two rival species. === Phase 2: Enslavement + Rebellion (May 1, 2005) === After the overwhelming defeat of the Sheep, the worms have thrown the sheeps into slavery. Many have fallen. 500 years later, Sheeps are executed, used as cheap labor, and cannon fodder. Morale weakened, the sheep give in. Suddenly, just when things couldn't get worse, a band of Sheep have reported to have joined forces with a mysterious, "Evil Sheep Lord". 1-out lives will be subracted from those who have been out. Choose an out person from each side. From the sheep side, He/She will be known as "Evil Sheep Lord". From the worm side, he/she will be known as "Worm Alpha". For the duration of the contest, he will be this. However, they only have 2 out-lives, and their old scores will be deleted. == Rules == Players had to use Skins respectively to whichever army they have belonged to. Sheep army had to use a Sheep themed Skin, made by [[Jukke]]. Liero Worm army had to use the LieroX worm, made by [[Strider]]. It was forbidden for contestants to use the Anti-Sheep Skin. Players on the same team were gathering together to defeat the opposing team. Each team could choose whoever they wanted to go with to the war. Each players had "3-out" lives which meant player could be out of the game in total of 3 times per whole Sheep Wars. In other words if a player got out in one battle, the next game he played, he could only get out 2 more times. Players were allowed to use any mod and loading time they liked. However, the level was being selected beforehand. There was no limit in how many players could play per one team. It could have been from 1 vs 7 to 4 vs 4. Duels between the contestants were allowed but limited to one per phase. === Point system equation === 
[Lives ^ (9/10) + Kills] = Contestant's points
== Players == === Sheep Team === * Anubis * Avye * Darky * Dflies * Duck * Enigma * Fazure * Gigolo * id815 * Jukke * Koin * Kurt Cobain * Navi * Morbid * Pupsi * Red Baron ([[Raziel]]) * Rejected * Voidstalk * Zapp * Zultan === Liero Team === * Adam * Beat * Cloud * d0t * Exigo * HedgeEx * Holzwurm * Jesus * Kray * Mr. Sandman * NaturaL * O2aNGE * Pent * Punk Zombie * QuafaS * Ronin * [[Ruki]] * Skillz * Tiger * TTFTCUTS * Worminator == Hall of fame == === Phase 1: Primal Aggression === * Sheep Champion: Zapp * Liero Champion: [[Ruki]] === Phase 2: Enslavement + Rebellion === N/A == External links == * [http://www.freewebs.com/sheepwars/index.htm Sheep Wars home page] * [http://www.thegaminguniverse.com/forums/showthread.php?t=21945 Sheep Wars GU thread] [[Category:Contests]]

'''SimpleX Modding team''' (active in 2004 - 2005) was a team formed by [[HocusPocus]] in June 2004 to work on various LieroX Mods and packs.
== Members == === Leader === * [[HocusPocus]] - (gfx, sfx, scripting, levels, skins)
=== Normal members === * Andy Tran - (gfx, skins) * [[Blackninja]] - (gfx, scripting, levels) * Colonel Darktale - (gfx, sfx, scripting) * Kossu Man - (scripting) * MasterOfHyrule - (sfx, levels) * Mauganra99 - (sfx, scripting) * NobodyX - (gfx, scripting) * Rad Tad - (gfx, sfx, scripting) * the new gd - (gfx, sfx, scripting) * TTFTCUTS - (gfx, scripting) * Tuplanolla - (gfx, sfx, scripting)
== Projects == === LieroX-Mas === LieroX-Mas Mod is a Mod with 34 Christmas related weapons. SimpleX Modding team also made Christmas theme Levels, Skins and Frontend which were released in [[Liero Xtreme v0.56 Christmas Edition]].
=== Unfinished === As a 2nd project SimpleX Modding team was planning to create LieroX Mod, a mod that would be a representative of the LX scripting capabilities. It was never finished or released. Same happened with their 3rd project - Anime Mod.
== External links == * [http://www.thegaminguniverse.com/forums/showthread.php?t=18568 SimpleX Modding team GU thread]

'''Single player''' support was added in [[OpenLieroX]]. It is intended mostly to provide tutorial levels for newcomers but it may also be used for more complex stuff. This probably will be extended a bit later on.
Single player games aren't really something new. They are just a list of scripted [[Gusanos Maps]]. Or you can also just setup any custom game for one game level.
[/forum/index.php/topic,12829.0.html Read here for some more information.]

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text:
== Skinning == Making custom skins relatively easy, although a complex skin is time-consuming to make. The skins in the game are located in the "skins" folder. To save time and ensure that your skin will be fully functional, follow these guidelines:
* Download the skin template below.
[[File:SkinTemplate.png]]
* Your skin will go into the top 672x36 pixels (the top 1/3rd as shown below). The rest of the template is for assistance only during the making process, and should be removed once the skin is finished.
[[File:Skinguide.png]]
* The skin consists of two rows with 21 frames in each, each frame being 25x18 pixels. The top row contains the version of the skin that will be seen ingame. The bottom row determines which colors in the corresponding drawing above are static and which are changable.
[[File:Skinlevels.png]]
* Each frame is designated by a pink box containing green bordering . The part of the green bordering that forms a box in each frame indicates the dimensions of the skin that interacts with solid areas and projectiles ingame; borders included. These may be used as a guide to centering the skin. The frames are separated by non-usable space designated by a dark pink color .
* The pink color (RGB:255,0,255) is transparent, and once the skin is finished, all assisting ready-made green and dark pink areas should be replaced with pink.
* The blue horizontal bar below the skin in the template contains the standard colors that should be used for areas that are supposed to change color. Using other close nuances of similar blue is likely to result in dysfunctional color changing. It is highly recommended to have color-changing areas because of team games where color indicates which team the player is on.
* Each of the two rows in the skin is divided into three sections of seven frames per section. The frames in the left section are shown while the skin is standing still. While the skin is moving, the frames are cycled from the left section, to the corresponding frame in the center section, and then on to the right, whereafter the cycle is repeated . These three frames are indicated by the same number in the skin template.
[[File:Skinsections.png]]
* Each frame in each section designates a particular aim of the skin, as indicated by the aim disk in the skin template. The blue section of the disk shows the degree of aim that a particular frame corresponds to.There are three frames for each aim, one in each section, and these will form the animation of the skin.
* The bottom row should correspond exactly to the upper row , except in terms of colors. The areas of the skin which aren't supposed to change color should be marked with black (RGB: 0, 0, 0) in the bottom row. Areas which are supposed to change color should be marked with white (RGB: 255, 255, 255). However, while you are making the skin, you can save time by having the bottom row identical to the top row. This will make it possible to observe the skin in action, although without the correct coloring. * When you start making the skin, make sure that it faces to the right. The program will automatically mirror it to the left. * Once you have finished making it, resize the template to remove all the assisting parts, so that that the skin has the dimensions 672x36 pixels. Replace the assisting dark pink and green coloring with the transparent pink color. * Name the skin according to skin naming standards such as "AssaultWorm" instead of "assaultworm" or "Assault worm". Save the file in .png format, and preferrably check that the extension becomes ".png" and not ".PNG". If the only option is .PNG, you can simply rename the skin and change the extension. * If you are going to upload the skin so that others may download it, it is not recommended to use imageshack.us to upload your skin. Imageshack will add a few random characters to the end of the name of the skin. The best option is to use [[FileBase]].
Useful template: [[File:Skin_template.png]]
[[Category:Guides]]
<!-- imported from Wayback Machine: https://web.archive.org/web/20130324065322/http://www.openlierox.net:80/wiki/index.php/Skin -->

[http://sourceforge.net SourceForge] is a free service to host an Open Source project and provides all kind of tools to work on it, like [[Git]], all the file release packages, the [[Mailinglist|mailinglists]] and so on.
[[OpenLieroX]] uses SourceForge since the very beginning for most of the [[Development|development]]. As of May 2011 though, we have moved the [[OpenLieroX source code|main code]] [[Git]] repository to [[GitHub]].

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text:
'''Top Clan League''' ('''TCL''') is manually edited forum-based Liero Xtreme clan ladder. It was created by [Osp] MileC in March 2008. Currently it consists of 9 clans.
== TCL System == TCL is a CW competition between its members. All member clans are given a rank from 1 to 3. A victory against a rank 3 clan gives 1 point, rank 2 two points and rank 1 three points. TCL Cws are played with 10 lives and best of three. TCL staff has also worked out basic weapon ban lists to standardize the cw playing. TCL members are listed manually by points and winning percentage. The list is updated by members theirselves and therefore is hard to fake.
== TCL Rules == * TCL matches are best of three * Rounds are played with 10 lives * Clans can have a maximum of 8 TCLCWs against eachother during one season (approx. 3-4 months) * TCL Matches can be played 2v2, 3v3 or 4v4 * All playing styles are accepted (except cheating)
== TCL Members == * [MaW] [[Masters @ Work]] (Rank 2) * [aC] [[Annuit Coeptis]] (Rank 2) * [NB-AT] [[Nigga Blasta-Assault Team]] (Rank 2) * [FoH] [[Flowers of Happiness]] (Rank 2) * [Osp] [[Oldschool Players]] (Rank 2) * [AoG] [[Army of Glory]] (Rank 2) * -oFaFo- [[-aFo- & -oFa-]] (Rank 2) * |LaG| [[Lame Ass Gaming]] (Rank 3) * [pFe] [[Pure Fucking Evil]] (Rank 3)
== Former Members == * [Uw] [[United Warriors]] (Rank 2) - Clan retired * {Q} [[Quality]] (Rank 3) - Clan left TCL
== TCL Moderating Team == * [Osp] MileC (Administrator) * [Osp] Pera (Administrator) * [Osp] Zapp (Administrator) * [AoG] Z01 (Administrator) * [aC] Legend (Moderator) * [MaW] 'Orch!D' (Moderator)
== Links == * [http://z9.invisionfree.com/lxtopclans/index.php TCL official page] * [http://z9.invisionfree.com/lxtopclans/index.php?showtopic=46&st=0 TCL CW results] * [http://z9.invisionfree.com/lxtopclans/index.php?showtopic=13 TCL official bannings]

revision.text:
Talking with the [[Developers|developers]] is the best way to get involved with the [[OpenLieroX]] project. There are multiple ways you can contact us. Best way is to leave a mail in our [[Mailinglist|mailinglist]], i.e. at [mailto:openlierox-devel@lists.sourceforge.net openlierox-devel@lists.sourceforge.net].
== Instant messaging / Chatting == Some contact details of the main developers:
* ''Albert'' ** Jabber/GTalk: albzey@gmail.com ** ICQ: 107220269 * ''Karel'' (alias DarkCharlie) ** ICQ: 241749823 ** MSN: karel.petranek@tiscali.cz
There is also [[IRC|an IRC room]], although it is not visited that often from the developers.
== Further links == Read more details about [[How to contribute to OpenLieroX|how to contribute to OpenLieroX]].
== Frequently asked questions == In many cases, people interested in the code of OLX are asking the same questions. I have copied some [[Typical developer introduction talk|chat excerpts which answer some common questions here]].

'''Team Omega Squadron''' ('''ToS_''') (May 2004 - October 2004) was mostly North American and Rifles setting playing clan. == History == Team Omega Squadron was already existing in other games like Junglewar, Vietcong, Hidden and Dangerous 2, Day of Defeat, Counter-Strike 2D and Call of Duty for a longer period (January in 2003 to be exact) when they decided to make a squadron in LieroX in May of 2004. It was a successful start as they soon became one of the best North American clans at that point. In July of 2004 some of the members tried to force other clan mates to change their playing style from Rifles only to Limited 100 lt and Multimodding which caused into most of the members leaving and therefore ToS had to reform the clan. 2nd period of the clan was born. After the Summer of 2004 there was a great lack of players and activity in ToS and it was time to retire the clan in LieroX in October of 2004. Team Omega Squadron still lives on today in other communities (including World of Warcraft). == Original Members == === Leader === * 0wner === Co-Leader === * Anders === Normal members === * l3lack Ninja * Big Red * Black Hawk * Death/Cloud * Maki * ooworm * [[Ruki]] * Samurai * Smugli/Dape * The Jeet * Tizzle == Members in 2nd period == === Leader === * 0wner === Co-Leader === * Death/Cloud === Normal members === * Badest Mofo * Big Red * Black Hawk * ooworm * RaW * Samurai * The Jeet == Clan Wars == === Won === * ToS_ vs [nn] (2:0) * ToS_ vs WsW_ (2:0) === Lost === * ToS_ vs [TK] (0:1) === Tied === N/A == External links == * [http://guildopedia.wikia.com/wiki/Team_Omega_Squadron Team Omega Squadron Guildopedia page] * [http://teamos.org/ Team Omega Squadron home page] * [http://www.geocities.com/coolsiteau/tosclan/hdhome.htm Team Omega Squadron old home page] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Retired clans]]

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text:
'''The Lobby''' (Abbr. '''TL''') (August 2005 - November 2006) was a forum originally founded in August 2005 by [[User AnubisBlessing|AnubisBlessing]] and Raziel, although quite soon after that Loop joined the staff and the activity continued on the current forums after being moved from the original location.
=== The story behind === Around the time of the creation of TL the bigger part of the LX community was based on [[Gaming Universe]], where there was general discontent against the moderating team of the forums. The players felt neglected and disrespected by the mods and admins, which led to bad air between the two parts and culminated in bans of members which were not approved of by the community. So a small group of users emigrated to make their own forums and try to convince the community that it was ripe to move out of the hosted forums on GU.
==== Features ==== The idea of TL was to create a forum for players by players, and try to move out the activity from GU. The most popular section of GU, the clan threads were was remade and TL offered hosted board to clans or competition hosts not willing to make an own forum that would end up having too little activity. Basically everything GU had was improved upon and focused to the benefit of the player. However, the TL community would suffer from lack of development as the then most active modders, skinners and level makers would still be based on GU. Even if what were considered the most important clans joined the activity on the forums, it would seem hard to compete with the GU. TL also implemented what was to be called the round table. Which was thought be be something like the alliance of LXA works now; a place for the the more established clans to exchange information and discuss events in the LX community. However it is questionable wether or not it worked as intended. But the biggest feature of all was the Filebase made by Loop and designed by Raziel.
=== The death of TL === In times of an already split community - GU vs TL, [[Classic]] versus [[Mortars]] - A new contender would appear on the forum arena. Made by thus far unknown players mainly from the mortar world it would end up consuming the activity on both GU and TL and prove to be the place where both mortar and classic players would coexist and eventually, the international forum scene would be dominated by the [[Liero Xtreme Alliance]]. In November 2006 TL was locked for public posting.
== See also == [[Gaming Universe]] [[Liero Xtreme Alliance]]
[[Category:Liero Xtreme Community]]
<!-- imported from Wayback Machine: https://web.archive.org/web/20130331042206/http://www.openlierox.net:80/wiki/index.php/The_Lobby -->

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text:
= The Palace Series = The palace like levels made by Legend between years 2007-2008. Levels usually lack original LieroXtreme level features, but on the other hand provide lots of unusual things. To get these levels either search from thelobby filebase or LXAlliance downloads.
== Palaces == * Desert Palace - Palace that started this serie. Background decision shares opinions, as the level overall does. Simple gameplay makes the level convenient to play with.
* Ancient Palace - Likewise Desert Palace, also Ancient Palace has loads of 90¬∞ angles. Level is pretty big, so gameplay is mostly fast and open.
* Water Palace - Gameplay is very open, like suitable for mortars. Contains rare humour from Legend, as the fishes' layot indicates.
* Ice Palace - The first really serious attempt, author's one of the favourites. Level is capable to handle big size battles.
* Shadow Palace - Doesn't really suit many players, as the game would be total chaos in this large size map. Optimal for tight 2vs2 battles.
* Great Palace - Big. Large. Massive. Huge. Are there more adjectives?
* Sacred Palace - Compared to previous levels there can be detected some development. Symmetric is the most predominating feature.
* Darkness Palace - Similar to Sacred Palace, though moving is a lot of more limited indoors. Completely dark background reminds a bit Ancient Palace. Completely symmetric again.
[[Category:Levels]]
<!-- imported from Wayback Machine: https://web.archive.org/web/20110721135823/http://www.openlierox.net/wiki/index.php/The_Palace_Serie -->

revision.text:
Notice
This article is about a clan that has been tagged dead. Any info is to be considered purely of historical value. Feel free to correct any inaccuracies.
'''Toilet Warriors''' ('''[wc]''') (March 2004 - June 2005) was one of the best and most successful clans when talking about competative side of LieroX. A lot of players, specially the older ones who originate from the time of Toilet Warriors era, claim that wc is the best clan to ever exist in the LieroX community and it will not be able to find a follower anytime soon. Members of the clan were mostly playing Liero v1.0 mod but they were open for other settings as well. Every 'Toileteer', as they would sometimes refer to themselves, was well known for being one of the most active and skilled players of that time. == History == Clan was formed by real life friends Kot, d0t and Dimon in March of 2004. The idea was to make some catchy and funny sounding name for a clan. Kot or d0t came up with the idea about Toilet Warriors and it was approved by all three guys instantly. The tag was generated later as the word toilet would often ring an association to a water closet. Originally Kot was the leader but he would soon retire from LieroX and leave the first place to Dimon, d0t was named for a co-leader. Dimon and d0t were the only members for quite some time until they got familiar with [[Rifles]] combo in a clan war versus [[Player-Killers]]. After that they started to play only [[Rifles]] and came across KW clan where they met Kalma. Kalma was very interested in the name Toilet Warriors and soon he has joined the clan making Toilet Warriors count three players in clan wars from that point. With Kalma the clan was spreading fast and in no time it would become a group of active and high skilled players. Last to join the clan were Beat and later Drama and Kaptengu, who were originally to play a series of duels in order to declare the one joining the clan. In the end it turned out both were very skilled, active and serious players so they both made it to the clan. Toilet Warriors retired from active playing in July of 2005. Reason for that was lack of time by most of the members and some of the members leaving their home land to have a further studies abroad. However not all the players left LieroX community. Some moved to another clans but beginning of the Summer in 2005 was a final chapter in the history of Toilet Warriors. Clan's last move was meant to be the Toilet Warriors Fairwell Tournament but it failed due the lack of maturness of contestants involved. Instead they left the community with a farewell movie tribute titled The Legacy Of Toilet Warriors. == Members == === Leader === * Dimon === Co-Leader === * d0t === Normal members === * Axl-Man * Beat * Drama * Kalma * Kaptengu * Kemp * Muhis === Early retired members === * Disturbed * Droplul * Kot * Rex * Striker XL == Clan Wars == === Won === 40 wars === Lost === 1 war === Tied === None === Statistics === * Longest winning streak: 62 games (10.4. '04 - 9.10. '04) * All time wins: 102 games * All time losses: 9 games * Total kill count: 2413 confirmed kills in clan wars * Total lives lost: 1739 deaths in clan wars * Kill/Death Ratio: 1.390 == Other achievements == * First place in Werrock's Clan Base since the day it was formed * Second place in Liero World Series 2004 * Toilet Wars level == The Legacy Of Toilet Warriors == "Well this is it. Our Legacy to LX. I have done quite a lot to make this a worthwile download. There is not much i can say about the movie, i let it speak for it self. Enjoy!" ''~Kalma'' [http://video.google.com/googleplayer.swf?docId=171026108084548938 The Legacy Of Toilet Warriors stream] === Enemy Of [wc] === This is a small tribute to Morbid for helping with creation of The Legacy Of Toilet Warriors by hosting most of the games which were being recorded. [http://video.google.com/googleplayer.swf?docId=-5730110906007514615 Enemy Of wc stream] == External links == * [http://www.thegaminguniverse.com/forums/showthread.php?t=16949 Toilet Warriors GU thread] * [http://www.thegaminguniverse.com/forums/showthread.php?t=22832 Toilet Warriors Farewell Tournament GU thread] * [http://video.google.com/googleplayer.swf?docId=171026108084548938 The Legacy Of Toilet Warriors stream] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Retired clans]]

Notice
This article is about a clan that has been tagged dead. Any info is to be considered purely of historical value. Feel free to correct any inaccuracies.
= |2JM| Two J's and an M = == History == At a night in a little village in the Netherlands, there were three boys who were drinkin' some beer, smokin' some spliffs, and talkin' about nonsense. These three boys were called Jordy (Screams), Jan remmelt (Sneaky) and Mart (Beat). While they continued talkin' nonsense the subject liero became pretty populair. After talkin' about all players in lx that anoyed them or wich they like they thought like:"we should make like a little clan with the three of us, wich we play with rarely and no one knows its us". (later on I told my fellow toiletteers but lets keep with the subject) Well in our by that time pretty drunk state we thought up the name 2JM, wich was comming from our three names. Then we started thinkin' about names... "WOA! We should be like a godlike clan!" shouted someone, dunno who it was. And that is how we all thought up our own name. After playing with those nick rarely like half a year [wc] died. That is the point I decided I would carry on with my 2JM nick, and so did sneaky and screams. And that my friends.. is how 2JM came to what it is now :) == Members == === Founders === * |2JM| Ra (Beat) * |2JM| Zeus * |2JM| Boedda === Regular members === * |2JM| Beast * |2JM| Fierce == Played levels & mods == === Mods === * Liero 1.0 - 100% & 20% * MSF - 100% * Modern Warefare - 100% === Levels === * Liero factory * Jail Break * Complex * Jukke dome * Fossic facillity == Rules == * NO double clanning * NO whining (zeus has some trouble with this) * NO Cw's without telling one of the leaders about it. * NO offensie language towards people who beat you * Give people a fight, also when you are losing * Be able to except you loss. == Clan wars == * |2JM| vs. [RaN] 2 - 0 * |2JM| vs. [Osp] 0 - 2 == Additional information == * |2JM| Ra used to be [wc] Beat, he will still play under that nick sometimes, so if your searching for a 2JM member and you see Beat, ask me. * |2JM| Zeus used to be Sneaky * |2JM| Boedda used to be screams. We changed names because we would be the clanleaders and we liked the idea as gods as names. The members will probably keep their own name when they join. * |2JM| Beast used to be Freedom" [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Retired clans]]

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text: I often feel that I repeat myself on every introduction for a developer newcomer. As this might be interesting for other people, I am copying here some excerpts of some discussions (of course with permission to do so). The chat excerpts itself are sometimes a bit edited/modified (if I talked bullshit for example). Feel free to correct/edit them yourself. If you have not already, read also the general [[Contribute to the source code|code contribute introduction article]]. 18:14:12 Albert Zeyer: you have access to the [[SourceForge|SF]] [[Git|git]] now 18:14:17 Kai Leddy: thanks 18:16:10 Albert Zeyer: do you have checked out the [[Source|source]] already? 18:16:54 Kai Leddy: not yet i havent but i will in a minute 18:23:52 Kai Leddy: ..., I can work on [[OpenLieroX]] whenever I dont have other work to do, ..., I found OpenLieroX through a friend, and have been playing it on and off for about a year and a half, and learnt c++ but didnt have any real projects to work on with it, and thought i should help work on an existing open source project, when i stumbled through my bookmarks and found openlierox again. 18:25:31 Albert Zeyer: ah cool :) yes, [[Openlierox|openlierox]] is a nice project to learn practical programming in c++ and some other languages, also including python (but only minor, only for the dedicated script) 18:25:43 Albert Zeyer: most of us learned a lot in this project 18:26:57 Kai Leddy: well, i am hoping to learn a lot from the project and at the same time help out an open source project, and at the same time see if i can get some friends interested in playing the game 18:28:10 Albert Zeyer: depending how much time&energy you are willing to spend on the project, you can learn a lot. such a project covers really a huge area of topics in computer science, both theoretical and practical 18:28:37 Albert Zeyer: i wrote an article about that once: [[Why game development is a great learning playground]] 18:29:56 Kai Leddy: is that the full list of libraries it uses, just so i can install them 18:30:34 Albert Zeyer: it may be a hurdle at the beginning to get into the project because the code base has become quite huge. but on the other side, you don't really need to know every detail about the code to work with it and understand it. an overview is enough and i put some documentation about the code, including a code overview, into our wiki 18:30:46 Albert Zeyer: what operating system do you use? 18:31:21 Kai Leddy: i mostly use ubuntu 10.04 64 bit, but i also have a partition with windows 7 again 64 bit 18:31:41 Albert Zeyer: ah good. to setup the development environment will be much easier on linux 18:32:02 Albert Zeyer: [[Compiling OpenLieroX on Linux]] 18:32:12 Kai Leddy: thats mostly why i use linux, i find its a lot easier to set up and use when i comes to coding 18:32:24 Albert Zeyer: it should work if you just execute that command from there, that should install all needed libs and stuff 18:32:36 Kai Leddy: ahh thanks 18:32:57 Albert Zeyer: and here for checking out from git: [[Obtaining OpenLieroX source from Git]] 18:33:25 Albert Zeyer: you should switch to the 0.59 branch as it is stated there 18:33:44 Albert Zeyer: ah btw, you should register in the mailinglist if you have not already so you get any dev mails 18:35:33 Kai Leddy: ok, i might need a bit of help with git in a bit, i havent used it before as i have only worked on small projects i never needed to use it 18:36:40 Albert Zeyer: it's also quite useful for small projects :) i use it all the time for just everything. also because it is so trivial to use (just a git init in some directory and i have put it under git control). in case of openlierox, just copy&paste the commands from the page to clone the repo 18:38:12 Kai Leddy: ok, thanks, im not exactly sure what the other git commands do though, such as checkout, pull, commit and things 18:38:31 Kai Leddy: but at least once i know how to use it it makes the project much easier to manage 18:39:42 Kai Leddy: im cloning the git repository at the moment, it seems to be taking a while 18:40:22 Albert Zeyer: just ask if you dont know sth 18:41:17 Albert Zeyer: but to only use it and do basic things, there is not much you must know except of the very basic commands, like commit, add, push, pull 18:41:41 Albert Zeyer: yea, cloning will take a while, it has become quite huge :) i think 300mb or sth 18:42:08 Albert Zeyer: that's because it contains the full history since the very beginning of the project and also all the data (like graphics, levels, etc) 18:42:24 Kai Leddy: oh, that explains it 18:43:16 Albert Zeyer: there is also a way to just clone the latest revision and ignore the other history, so i think you would probably save 50% (not much actually if you take into account that we have about 8000 revisions) 18:43:48 Kai Leddy: well its 70% done now so i might as well just let it finish 18:43:49 Albert Zeyer: but i think it is worth to have everything. sometimes looking at the history is very helpful, esp when searching for errors but also for learning why someone made some change or so 18:44:15 Kai Leddy: so have you been working on openlierox since the beginning of the project? 18:44:29 Albert Zeyer: yea. karel and i have founded the project 18:45:32 Kai Leddy: its been going for a long time now, its a really fun game to play and in the time i have been playing it even it has grown quite a bit 18:45:55 Albert Zeyer: the project openlierox. olx source code is based on the lierox project. lierox was closed source. and then the developer of it (only one guy, jason boettcher or jasonb) has lost interest in it, stopped working on it and published the source code. that is where olx started 18:46:18 Kai Leddy: ah 18:46:22 Albert Zeyer: but the source code has changed quite a lot since then. and also grown a lot. i think it has grown by 300% or so 18:47:01 Albert Zeyer: the git history starts with mostly this original lierox version 18:47:26 Kai Leddy: i never played the original lierox but openlierox i think is an amazing game and i think its sort of a shame more people dont play it 18:47:50 Kai Leddy: everyone i played the game with or showed it to out of my friends loved it 18:48:35 Albert Zeyer: the gameplay of olx is just the same, in fact it is just the same game as lierox. ofc many new stuff has been added over the time, so some people might call it a different game now but the standard settings when you start a new game are mostly just like the original lierox 18:51:40 Albert Zeyer: a few times in the past, some changes has been made to the physics engine of the original lierox code (mostly to fix some bugs) but the community of oldschool players was mostly very annoyed by that, also because of the frequent changes, so that we tried to recover the original code as far as possible in the recent time and made many things optionable (so you have options like "enable LX56 buggy wallshoot behaviour" and more) 18:52:25 Albert Zeyer: 0.58rc3 is the latest stable version we have published. the latest beta is 0.59beta9. since 0.59, we have recovered the exact physics code from lx56 and made almost everything configureable 18:53:34 Kai Leddy: nice 18:54:43 Albert Zeyer: do you know gusanos? -> [[Gusanos]] 18:55:09 Albert Zeyer: do you know the original liero? (not lierox, i mean the real original liero) -> [[Liero]] 18:55:17 Kai Leddy: no, i saw something about it on the openlierox website but i dont know what it is 18:55:37 Kai Leddy: no, i never knew about liero until openlierox 18:56:09 Albert Zeyer: the original liero was a DOS game, already quite old (created in 1998) 18:56:13 Albert Zeyer: and the most famous 18:56:45 Albert Zeyer: lierox was the most famous clone of it. mostly because it was easy to use and run on windows 18:56:58 Albert Zeyer: [[Lierox|lierox]] started in 2002 18:57:13 Albert Zeyer: gusanos also started in 2002. whereby they basically recoded it from scratch (or with at least major changes) in each version. gusanos 0.9, was released firstly in 2005. gusanos 0.9c with many more bugfixes to 0.9 was released 2006. 18:57:45 Albert Zeyer: gusanos is also a liero clone. the gusanos physics is much more close to the original liero than in lierox 18:57:55 Albert Zeyer: and the gusanos engine is also much more nice after all 18:58:23 Albert Zeyer: problem was that in the beginning, gusanos did not have any menu, so you had to do everything via the ingame console (first version with a menu was 0.9) 18:58:34 Albert Zeyer: also, the network engine in gusanos sucked a bit 18:58:44 Albert Zeyer: gusanos was always open source, since the very beginning 18:59:23 Albert Zeyer: but a problem was that it depends on commercial libs, so it was not really possible to port it to new platforms. only systems it supported were windows and a few linux 32bit x86 19:00:03 Albert Zeyer: i have done some work a year ago or so to replace all the libs in gusanos by open ones (and recode some missing functionality) 19:00:16 Albert Zeyer: and then i merged the whole gusanos code into openlierox 19:00:56 Albert Zeyer: so we have both physics engines in olx now. and not only physics engines, also the modding engine and scripting and stuff. gusanos uses lua 19:01:26 Albert Zeyer: well, i have written an article about the details why i did that and about the benefits etc in the wiki, read that if you are interested -> [[Gusanos in OpenLieroX]] 19:02:30 Kai Leddy: well its sounds like you have done a lot of work, merging gusanos with openlierox must have taken ages 19:02:58 Albert Zeyer: yea was not that easy. but also not impossible as some other people have said earlier :) 19:03:29 Albert Zeyer: and it's also not fully finished yet. it works in 0.59 but there are quite a few issues 19:04:45 Albert Zeyer: sometimes a bit unstable (but most of these problems are fixed now). biggest problem is maybe the network engine, which still sucks in gusanos (i have embedded the old gusanos net engine into the OLX net protocol, so there is also quite some overhead and the general handling is a bit hacky) 19:05:05 Kai Leddy: ah ok 19:05:27 Albert Zeyer: there are some plans to rewrite the full net engine. i think that is probably the only good solution to solve all the problems 19:05:48 Albert Zeyer: some initial work was already done for this, though it's unfinished 19:06:43 Albert Zeyer: but this is maybe some of the most wanted features the community wants. a new nice network engine. even in LX, it is not perfect (never was). it is not perfectly synced 19:08:51 Albert Zeyer: well, it would take some time to explain everything about this in detail. maybe compile it first, decide where you want to work on and if it is the net engine, i can give you more details about it :) but the net engine is probably also the most complicated and difficult thing you could work on, so i dont know if it is really a good idea to start with this :) you should also first read the current network code and look at the unfinished work pelya has done for a new net engine -> [[Network engine]] 19:11:29 Kai Leddy: ok, well i tried to compile it and it failed with all sorts of errors, it seems because im missing a few dependencies which i found in DEPS, so im installing them then im going to try to compile again 19:13:14 Albert Zeyer: ok. you probably also can tell from the error messages. always check out the very first error it gives you, also following ones are probably related. it's most often sth like #include XY, cannot find XY 19:16:08 Kai Leddy: ok i cant work out why it wont compile 19:16:17 Albert Zeyer: what's the very first error? 19:16:20 Kai Leddy: ill send you the terminal output 19:16:25 Albert Zeyer: just the first line 19:16:28 Albert Zeyer: of the error 19:16:47 Kai Leddy: /home/kai/Projects/OpenLieroX/libs/breakpad/src/common/linux/dump_symbols.cc: In function ‘void::FixAddress(void*)’: 19:16:57 Albert Zeyer: ok also the next line :) 19:17:04 Albert Zeyer: the error itself 19:17:13 Albert Zeyer: ah, you have a 64bit system? 19:17:23 Kai Leddy: /home/kai/Projects/OpenLieroX/libs/breakpad/src/common/linux/dump_symbols.cc:83: error: cast from ‘void*’ to ‘Elf64_Word’ loses precision make[2]: *** [CMakeFiles/openlierox.dir/libs/breakpad/src/common/linux/dump_symbols.o] Error 1 make[1]: *** [CMakeFiles/openlierox.dir/all] Error 2 make: *** [all] Error 2 19:17:28 Albert Zeyer: yea 19:17:28 Kai Leddy: thats the entire error 19:17:35 Kai Leddy: and yeah i have 64bit 19:17:56 Albert Zeyer: do you have any problems if i publish parts of this chat discussion? :) because many things i have said here may be interesting to other newcomers as well 19:18:12 Kai Leddy: no you can if you want 19:18:47 Kai Leddy: if other people have the same problem it would be useful for them to know how i sorted it out 19:19:13 Albert Zeyer: do this: cmake -DBREAKPAD=Off . 19:19:17 Albert Zeyer: and then make again 19:20:07 Albert Zeyer: we use [[Google Breakpad]] for [[Automatic crash reports|automatic crash reports]]. however, google breakpad doesnt work on linux 64bit yet (or at least the version we have included in the code, i should check a newer version at some time). but as it is optional, it is not really a problem 19:20:24 Kai Leddy: oh ok thanks 19:21:03 Kai Leddy: it seems to be going fine now 19:22:17 Albert Zeyer: i extended the wiki for that bit :) 19:22:24 Kai Leddy: ok 19:22:38 Kai Leddy: i would not have guessed that because i have never even heard of breakpad 19:22:59 Kai Leddy: its compiling fine now, just a few warnings 19:23:44 Albert Zeyer: ok good 19:24:18 Kai Leddy: its just the occasional unused variable 19:24:50 Albert Zeyer: breakpad is cool, really nice and powerful lib. also used in projects like firefox and many others 19:25:30 Kai Leddy: oh ok 19:25:48 Kai Leddy: yeah the warnings are all just unused variables 19:26:55 Albert Zeyer: oh ok. should check that later on. it's also not always our code. we have some more libs included, for example libzip, hawknl or lua which may also be compiled along with olx (depending on your settings). and i dont really want to change that code 19:27:14 Kai Leddy: ok ... 19:55:31 Kai Leddy: where should i post it 19:55:41 Kai Leddy: in which mailing list 19:56:38 Albert Zeyer: openlierox-devel 19:56:47 Albert Zeyer: where you have posted your other mail 19:56:52 Kai Leddy: to be honest im not quite sure how to use mailinglists either 19:56:53 Albert Zeyer: have you registered in that list already? 19:57:30 Albert Zeyer: well, just register on it and then each time when someone sends a mail to openlierox-devel@lists.sourceforge.net, you will receive it 19:57:36 Albert Zeyer: that is all about mailinglists :) 19:57:41 Kai Leddy: oh ok 19:57:46 Kai Leddy: it seems very simple 19:58:25 Albert Zeyer: yea. and usually, when you hit reply in your mailclient to such a mail, it will automatically put that mail from the list as the receiver again, so that everyone also will get the answer 19:58:44 Kai Leddy: oh ok, nice 19:59:02 Kai Leddy: do i register to the mailinglist on sourceforge then? 19:59:49 Albert Zeyer: read here: [[Mailinglists]] 19:59:54 Albert Zeyer: there are the register links 20:00:21 Kai Leddy: thanks ... 20:16:06 Albert Zeyer: yea those are mostly dead links. articles which have not been written yet 20:16:15 Albert Zeyer: i am getting an edit-new-article page when i click on them 20:16:26 Kai Leddy: oh ok ... 20:20:12 Albert Zeyer: the non finished articles in the wiki article [[OpenLieroX source code#overview_of_the_code|OpenLieroX source code#Overview_of_the_code]] are probably not that important, you will also get a good impression by just taking a short look at that code file 20:20:44 Albert Zeyer: all the points under "most important code" refer to some c++ classes or cpp files 20:20:54 Kai Leddy: ok

Notice
This article is about a clan that has been tagged dead. Any info is to be considered purely of historical value. Feel free to correct any inaccuracies.
= [Uw] United Warriors = == History == United warriors was born when two clans, WoF and BH45 decided to ally and merge. BH45 was going to die when almost all active members retired, and WoF didn't have too much active members either. We got an idea to ally from [Osp] MiLeC: "Well you could make pretty nice clan with great potential if you allied with [WoF], both are fast developing clans and together there could be start of great clan.". That's what he said in BH45 thread and the result was United warriors. == Senior Members == * [Uw] Xandoz * [Uw] Agn * [Uw] Flare * [Uw] oSq == Regular Members == * [Uw] Svipdag * [Uw] QuiverS * [Uw] Origin == Retired, kicked members == * Zepe * Jalmo * druFF * Da^Blue * Data * C.umi * Samara * QuafaS * Drama * Legend * Oxidi * Namura == Hosts == * Origin * Xandoz * Agn == Clan War Settings == * Liero v1.0, 100lt (limited weapons), non-combo * Liero v1.0, 20lt (rifle only), combo * Liero v1.11, 100lt (limited weapons), non-combo * Liero v1.11, 100lt (mines and zimm banned), non-combo * Marine Special Forces (MSF) II, 100lt (all weapons), non-combo * Modern Warfare1.0, 100lt (few weapons banned), non-combo * Shock v1.40 Air (limited weapons), non-combo * 8-bit warfare v1.2, 100lt (few weapons banned), non-combo * Food fight v0.6, 100lt (few weapons banned), non-combo * Trick or Treat v0.3 and 0.4, 100lt (skeleton cannon,witch hunt and black light banned), non-combo * Zelda v0.8, 100lt (some weapons banned), non-combo * Wh40k v0.13, 100lt (all weapons), non-combo == Preferred Levels == * Liero factory (revisited) * Fossil facility * Jail break * Lieros of Caribbean * Campus and all other... == Rules == * No camping, running, cheating etc. * No multiclanning * Senior members can start clan wars and recruit trials * Regular members can participate clan wars * Trial members can't participate clan wars (Unless seniors wants them to play) == Recruiting == If you want to be a member: * You must be good player in many mods * You must be great guy * You must be active * You must be loyal * All seniors have to accept new members == Links == [http://z9.invisionfree.com/lxtopclans/index.php?showtopic=33 Uw CW Results] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Retired clans]]

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text: '''The Basic User Page''' '''How(to)''' In your user page (click your name under the box that has the header '''Personal Tools''' to your right.), click edit and paste this code in: ``` `` {{UserPage| |Name=Your Name |Location=Your Location |DOB=Date of Birth |Date=Started Playing |Set=Favorite Setting |Nicks= Other nicks |Clans= Clans |Files=Files you created |Awards=Your awards |Other=Any other information about yourself you would like to add. }} `` ``` Edit all the information after the equal signs. You can space and enter between each line, so its possible to have this: ``` `` |Nicks= *Nickname *Nickname 2 *Nickname 3 |Awards= *1st place here *1st place there. Other= Lots of text Lots and lots of text Notice how you can keep going. `` ``` [[Category:Moderation]]

revision.comment: Imported from Internet Archive Wayback Machine
revision.text:
All ingame settings can be accessed via variables. To get a list, currently the easiest way is to use autocompletion in [[Ingame console|ingame console]] on the "setVar" command or with [[Chat commands|chat commands]] on the "/setVar" command. You can also take a look into [[Options.cfg|options.cfg]] to get a list. Some common variables: 
GameOptions.GameInfo.TimeLimit - the time limit in minutes
GameOptions.Network.ServerName - the servername
